ppa_notes: Store-and-forward: 256x8 byte buffer (mem[0:255]) inferred as RAM is the dominant area structure; localparam MSG_CAP=256, MAX_MSG default 192 <= MSG_CAP. Single-clock byte-serial datapath, one byte per egress beat, so no wide combinational fan-in. Likely critical path is the egress mux (header byte composition vs mem[rd_ptr] read) plus m_ready qualification; it is shallow and single-cycle as the contract requires. Highest-toggle nodes are rd_ptr/wr_ptr byte pointers, pay_left/bytes_remaining counters, and the egress data mux. No pipeline added (simple leaf peripheral); single-cycle APB response with pready tied high per contract. Explicit register write enables via FSM-gated NBAs let synthesis infer enables; no manual clock gating. Length/oversize compare is a 16-bit magnitude comparator against csr_max_msg. CSR read path is a flat address-decoded mux (combinational prdata). No oversize buffering risk: oversize is flagged on the beat that would exceed MAX_MSG, so wr_ptr cannot exceed MSG_CAP. Tradeoff: store-and-forward adds intake latency and a full-message buffer but is the simplest structure that guarantees CONTRACT_TX_ERR no-partial-emission; a cut-through design was rejected because it cannot retract a started packet on a late underrun/oversize.
domain_crossing_notes: Single clock domain clk; no CDC crossings (domain_intent.yaml clock_domains/cdc_crossings empty). rst_n is active-low, async-assert / sync-deassert: implemented with a 2-FF reset deassertion synchronizer (rst_meta -> rst_sync_n) clocked by clk and asynchronously cleared by rst_n, so assertion is immediate and deassertion is aligned to clk. Note: rst_sync_n is provided as the synchronized-deassert qualifier; the datapath/CSR flops use the async-assert pattern always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) per the OAG SV-lite dialect and the frozen interface. RDC: no_known_rdc for v0 per domain_intent.yaml (single reset). oag_domain_crossing_check.py --ip-dir mctp_tx_assembler --rtl-file rtl/mctp_tx_assembler.v -> status pass, 0 issues, clk/rst_n detected, 3 async-event blocks. No coherent multi-bit CDC exists, so no synchronizer/Gray/handshake mitigation is required. CDC/RDC closure is NOT claimed here; release-grade closure requires static/formal evidence per oag-cdc-rdc rules.
checks_run: iverilog 12.0 (stable). Parse/elaborate (no DUT change required): `cd mctp_tx_assembler && iverilog -g2012 -t null -Wall rtl/mctp_tx_assembler.v` -> exit 0, no errors/warnings. `iverilog -t null -Wall rtl/mctp_tx_assembler.v` (default, no -g2012) -> exit 0, no errors/warnings. OAG dialect/PPA screen: `python3 .claude/scripts/oag_ppa_check.py mctp_tx_assembler/rtl/mctp_tx_assembler.v --json` -> status=pass, dialect=oag_sv_lite_v1, fail=0 warn=0 issues=0. OAG domain crossing screen: `python3 .claude/scripts/oag_domain_crossing_check.py --ip-dir mctp_tx_assembler --rtl-file mctp_tx_assembler/rtl/mctp_tx_assembler.v --json` -> status=pass, 0 issues, rtl_files=1, clk + rst_n detected, 3 async-event blocks. Developer-only directed smoke sims (written to /tmp, NOT part of deliverable/evidence, then deleted; expected values hand-derived from behavior_model/cycle_rules, independent of DUT): (1) 130B msg @ MTU64 -> 3 packets (64,64,2), header bytes 01/AB(dest)/55(src)/byte3 layout, SOM/EOM=1,0,0/0,0,1, SEQ=0,1,2, TO/TAG preserved, irq_done pulse, SENT_CNT=1; zero-length (s_empty&s_last) -> ERR_FLAGS.zero_len=1, irq_err pulse, DROP_CNT=1; result SMOKE PASS. (2) race-free deterministic backpressure (m_ready low 2/5 cycles): 100B then 50B back-to-back messages emit in correct byte order across packets with no loss/duplication and correct SINGLEMSG gating (msg2 bytes only after msg1 drained); result SMOKE3 PASS. These smoke runs are debug confidence only; closure-grade scoreboard evidence is owned by the TB/sim agents.
summary: Implemented single-file synthesizable RTL mctp_tx_assembler.v (483 lines) realizing all 9 locked CONTRACT_TX_* contracts against the frozen interface doc. Store-and-forward FSM (IDLE->INTAKE->VALIDATE->EMIT_HDR->EMIT_PAY->DONE, plus inline error handling) buffers the whole message into mem[0:255], validates zero-length / oversize / underrun BEFORE any egress byte, then fragments into ceil(len/MTU) packets (short final packet), composes the DSP0236 4-byte header (byte0=0x01, byte1=dest_eid, byte2=CSR SRC_EID, byte3={SOM,EOM,SEQ[1:0],TO,MSG_TAG[2:0]}) followed by order-preserved payload, and asserts SOM/EOM and 2-bit SEQ (0 then +1 mod 4). s_ready stays low for a new message until the cycle after the EOM packet's m_eop completes (ST_DONE->ST_IDLE), giving CONTRACT_TX_SINGLEMSG. Egress holds m_valid/m_data while m_ready=0 (no byte lost). CSR map matches the frozen doc exactly (CTRL/SRC_EID/MTU/MAX_MSG/STATUS/IRQ_EN/ERR_FLAGS/SENT_CNT/DROP_CNT) with W1C on STATUS done/err and ERR_FLAGS, pready tied high, reset defaults SRC_EID=0x00, MTU=0x40, MAX_MSG=0x00C0. irq_done/irq_err are gated single-cycle pulses; SENT_CNT++ on EOM emission, DROP_CNT++ on each error. Reset is async-assert/sync-deassert active-low with reset values per CONTRACT_TX_RESET. No ontology/spec truth was modified; only rtl/ was written. Closure, lint signoff, coverage, and final completion are NOT claimed (may_claim_complete=false) and remain owned by the lint/sim/gate agents.
